input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the standard approach for runtime theme switching in .NET MAUI:

## 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

```xml
<!-- Resources/Styles/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="TextPrimary">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Create matching `DarkTheme.xaml` and `HighContrastTheme.xaml` with the same keys but different values.

## 2. Merge a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <local:LightTheme />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

## 3. Create a theme service to swap dictionaries at runtime

```csharp
public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(string themeName)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        ResourceDictionary theme = themeName switch
        {
            "Dark"         => new DarkTheme(),
            "HighContrast" => new HighContrastTheme(),
            _              => new LightTheme()
        };

        mergedDicts.Add(theme);

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("AppTheme", themeName);
    }
}
```

## 4. Use `DynamicResource` in your XAML (critical)

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
    <Label TextColor="{DynamicResource TextPrimary}" Text="Hello" />
</ContentPage>
```

`DynamicResource` (not `StaticResource`) ensures values update immediately when the dictionary changes.

## 5. Wire up the settings page

```csharp
// In your settings view model or code-behind
void OnThemeSelected(string choice) => ThemeService.ApplyTheme(choice);
```

## 6. Restore on startup

In `App.xaml.cs`:

```csharp
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(Preferences.Get("AppTheme", "Light"));
}
```

**Key points:**
- Same resource keys across all three dictionaries keeps consumption uniform.
- `DynamicResource` is what makes the swap instant without page reloads.
- `Preferences` persists the selection across app launches.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking how to set up themed styling in a .NET MAUI app with three branded themes (light, dark, high-contrast) that can be switched at runtime from a settings page. This is a general guidance/how-to question, not a code change request against a specific repo. I'll provide a concise explanation with the key architectural pieces.
